Federal Interagency TBI Research

About the dataset

Primary description: Traumatic brain injury data repository

The Federal Interagency TBI Research (FITBIR) informatics system is hosted in collaboration between the NIH and the UK Department of Defense, as a public dataset for research into traumatic brain injury (TBI). The consortium includes multiple studies with varying data collection procedures, all focused on TBI. Data is publicly available via [https://fitbir.nih.gov/].
Study Federal Interagency TBI Research
Study Website FITBIR
Data Descriptor Paper https://doi.org/10.1891/0739-6686.33.1
DUA https://fitbir.nih.gov/content/access-data
Country/Region USA
Disease Tag tbi
Age (mean ± SD)
(Baseline) 		33.3 ± 14.6
Age Range
(Baseline) 		17.04-86
% Female 28.5
CUBIC Project /cbica/projects/fitbir
PI for CUBIC Access Approval Christos Davatzikos
N Subjects 1500
N MR Sessions 3347
Diagnoses

Value V01 V02 V03 V04 V05 V06 V07 V08 V09 V10
TBI 978 807 161 94 17 14 11 6 2 1
CN 439 376 194 128
nan 79 34

nan 518 410 194 128

Data consolidated and harmonized in 2020.

This study: subset with df[df.Study == 'FITBIR']

Data consolidated and harmonized in 2026.

This study: subset with df[df.Study == 'FITBIR']


Funding
The Federal Interagency Traumatic Brain Injury Research (FITBIR) Informatics System is supported through a federal interagency collaboration primarily between the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD).

Get Support
